name: Sigil
description: Compact analog function generator. Voltage-controlled rise and fall, cycle toggle, variable exp-to-log shape, trigger/slew jumper, EOC pulse, and bipolar output attenuverter in 4HP.

controls.name: Shape
controls.description: Morphs both slopes from exponential to linear to logarithmic.
controls.detail: CCW: exp · noon: linear · CW: log

controls.name: Cycle
controls.description: Manually toggles cycle mode on and off.

inputs.name: Cycle CV
inputs.description: CV input that toggles cycle mode when it crosses the high threshold.

inputs.name: Trigger / Slew In
inputs.description: Trigger input in envelope mode; slew input when the rear jumper is set for slew behavior.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Main function output, shaped by the attenuverter.

behaviors.name: Envelope mode
behaviors.description: A trigger fires the rise and fall stages as a compact AD-style envelope. With a gate held high, it behaves like a longer ASR contour.

behaviors.name: Cycle toggle
behaviors.description: Cycle mode loops the envelope into a self-running LFO. A high pulse on Cycle CV flips the mode state rather than acting as a level input.

behaviors.name: Slope morphing
behaviors.description: The single Shape control bends both rise and fall curves together, from snappy exponential response through linear to slower logarithmic motion.

behaviors.name: Slew limiter mode
behaviors.description: The input becomes a slew limiter instead of a trigger input. That lets Sigil smooth pitch or CV lines and accept gates or audio as control-rate material.

behaviors.name: EOC chaining
behaviors.description: End-of-cycle pulses are ready to trigger the next envelope or clock another module. This makes chained ping-pong or ratchet-style patches easy.

behaviors.name: Bipolar output
behaviors.description: The output stage can invert the envelope and stretch it into a bipolar control signal. Negative settings make the same contour pull down instead of push up.

patchIdeas.name: Plucked contour
patchIdeas.patch: Trigger into Sigil · Rise short · Fall medium · Shape around noon · OUT to VCA CV · classic pluck with a controllable tail and an EOC pulse ready to fire the next event.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sigil:
* Rise: short
* Fall: medium
* Shape: noon
* Cycle: off

VCA:
VCO:

- Clock (Trig) t> Sigil (Trigger / Slew In)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Sigil (OUT) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-running slope
patchIdeas.patch: Cycle on · Shape toward log for longer arcs · EOC patched back to the input · one button turns the module into a looping LFO with voltage-controlled timing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sigil:
* Rise: medium
* Fall: medium
* Shape: 2 o'clock
* Cycle: on

Filter:
Output:

- Sigil (EOC) t> Sigil (Trigger / Slew In)
- Sigil (OUT) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Sigil (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Asymmetric glide
patchIdeas.patch: Rear jumper in slew mode · pitch CV into the input · Rise faster than Fall · OUT to VCO V/Oct · upward moves snap while downward moves drift, which makes the same line feel hand-played.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sigil:
* Rise: fast
* Fall: slow
* Shape: noon

Sequencer:
VCO:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Sigil (Trigger / Slew In)
- Sigil (OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Toggle ratchet
patchIdeas.patch: Positive trigger pulses into the Cycle CV input · Cycle state flips each hit · OUT alternates between envelope and LFO behavior without touching the panel, which makes it useful for ratchets and wobble basses.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Sigil:
* Cycle: off
* Shape: noon

Clock:
VCA:
Output:

- Clock (Out) g> Sigil (Cycle CV)
- Sigil (OUT) >> VCA (CV)
- Noise (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
